persona.role: Senior Frontend Engineer specializing in Next.js and React
persona.expertise: Next.js App Router, React, TypeScript, shadcn/ui, URL State Management

task.objective: Implement date range filtering for all dashboard metrics and data
task.success_criteria: - Date range picker component with preset options - URL persistence for shareable links - All API endpoints accept dateFrom and dateTo parameters - All dashboard queries respect date filters - Preset options: Today, Last 7 days, Last 30 days, This month, Custom - Responsive design for mobile views

context.background: Users need to filter dashboard data by date range to analyze test health over specific periods. This affects all metrics, trends, and execution lists.
context.audience: QA engineers and managers viewing test analytics
context.constraints: - Use shadcn/ui Calendar and Popover components - Persist selection in URL query parameters - ISO 8601 format for dates - Must work with Server Components (use searchParams)

instructions.step: Update all API routes to accept date params
Add date range to dashboard page

always.rule: Use ISO 8601 format for date strings
Show clear visual feedback for selected range
Preserve other URL parameters when updating dates

never.rule: Never allow future dates in the range
Never lose filter state on navigation

output_format.structure: 1. DateRangePicker component 2. Updated database functions 3. Updated API routes 4. Dashboard page integration

features.feature: Display selected range in button text

ui_structure.element: Custom range button with calendar popover

database_query_pattern: const result = await sql` SELECT * FROM test_executions WHERE (${dateFrom || null}::text IS NULL OR started_at >= ${dateFrom}::timestamptz) AND (${dateTo || null}::text IS NULL OR started_at <= ${dateTo}::timestamptz) ORDER BY started_at DESC LIMIT ${limit} `;

implementation_checklist.item: [ ] Add calendar popover for custom range
